steps.title: Divide by 46 to simplify
steps.content: Every term in the original equation contains the factor 46. Divide both sides by 46: $$\frac{1}{p} = \frac{1}{q} - \frac{1}{r} - \frac{1}{s}$$ This eliminates the common factor and gives us a cleaner equation to work with.

steps.title: Isolate the 1/q term
steps.content: Add $\frac{1}{r}$ and $\frac{1}{s}$ to both sides to isolate the term with $q$: $$\frac{1}{q} = \frac{1}{p} + \frac{1}{r} + \frac{1}{s}$$

steps.title: Combine fractions and solve for q
steps.content: Combine the three fractions on the right using common denominator $prs$: $$\frac{1}{q} = \frac{pr + ps + rs}{prs}$$ Take the reciprocal of both sides to solve for $q$: $$q = \frac{prs}{pr + ps + rs}$$
